learning_outcomes.description: Explain the rules and principles of law relating to the creation of trusts, and the duties, powers, rights and liabilities of trustees and persons in fiduciary positions.
Apply those rules and principles to concrete fact situations.
Read and analyse case and statute law in these areas of law.
Undertake research in these areas of law with a view to resolving more complex problems or critically evaluating the law.

assessments.task_description:
The assignment will be due on Thursday 3 September at 2pm (which is in Week 6).

The assignment is worth 50% of the total marks for the course.

This is a research assignment. It is not intended to be fully answerable using materials covered in lectures and tutorials.

The word limit is 2,500. This does not include footnotes (but it does include headings). However, footnotes must only contain references. Any substantive text in the footnotes will be disregarded.

The word count must be stated at the beginning of your answer. Your mark will be reduced by an amount equal to 10% of the available marks if your answer is between 1–50 words over the limit, 20% of the available marks if it is between 51–100 words over the limit, and so on.

References must comply with the 4th edition of the Australian Guide to Legal Citation. You do not need to include a bibliography.

Do not put your name on your answer, only your student number. This is to facilitate anonymous marking.

Do not work in groups, as this can amount to 'academic misconduct' as defined in the Student Code of Conduct Policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignments must be submitted electronically via the submission link on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The exam is open-book. All content covered in lectures, tutorials and readings is potentially assessable. More information about the content and format of the exam may be given closer to the exam date.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 12(27 Jul - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

The schedule of lectures is set out in the course Learning Guide. This is available from the course Blackboard site.

The lecture topics are:

  1. Introduction and Certainty Requirements
  2. Charitable Trusts
  3. Non-Charitable Purpose Trusts, Formality Requirements and Legality Requirements
  4. Complete Constitution and Equitable Assignments
  5. Terminating and Varying Trusts
  6. Trustees I
  7. Trustees II
  8. Fiduciary Duties
  9. Resulting Trusts
  10. Constructive Trusts
  11. Remedies I
  12. Remedies II

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
